issues: Workflow-capability contradiction on the three ratio canvases (steps 13-18): the outputs promise 1080x1080, 1080x1350 and 1080x1920 frames where the product is 'never cropped', 'preserved', with 'generous brand negative space' added on a solid #F4EFE7 canvas at the exact platform size, produced with 'no generation'. But the chosen tools cannot do this. image_crop_and_resize only crops/resizes the existing frame (it removes pixels or distorts; it cannot ADD canvas area to change a 4:3 hero into a 9:16 vertical while keeping the whole product). image_fill_area is solid-color-fill of a selection WITHIN the existing image (per reference_adobe_connector_exec_modes.md line 18) and cannot enlarge the canvas or place a product layer onto a new larger canvas. Extending canvas while preserving the full subject is either generative_expand (banned) or compositing, and the same memory (line 19) states compositing is not available headless. So done_creative_1x1/4x5/9x16 as specified cannot be produced by this workflow.

Internal contradiction inside the ratio steps: step 16's note asserts 'no canvas is invented, padded, or extended' and 'a crop/resize of the existing frame', while step 18's note and output spec (line 61) require 'leaving generous brand negative space' on a '1080x1920' canvas with the product preserved. You cannot both keep the full 4:3 product uncropped AND fill out to 9:16 without extending the canvas — the two descriptions are mutually exclusive.

The reverify block claims the gen-free reframe-and-place approach is valid ('places the product on a solid brand off-white canvas at the exact platform size'), but that placement operation (compositing product onto a larger solid canvas) is not something any listed headless tool performs — the fix that removed generative_expand replaced it with tools that also cannot achieve the stated output.

Step 12 tool choice is questionable: it uses image_fill_area to 'place the finished hero on a solid pure-white 5000x2800px field.' image_fill_area is a solid-color region fill on an existing raster, not a canvas-extension/compositing op. Going from a ~4000x5000 portrait hero to a WIDER 5000x2800 landscape white field requires enlarging/re-canvasing the image and positioning the product on it — that is padding/reframe work better expressed via image_crop_and_resize (fit-onto-white) than fill_area. As written the step does not obviously produce the stated wider-than-source canvas without either generative expand (correctly banned) or a compositing step the connector does not own. Recommend switching step 12 to image_crop_and_resize (pad-to-white to 5000x2800) or clarifying how fill_area yields a larger landscape canvas from a portrait source.
